tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent at $102.52/bbl on Mar 18 — steady as markets price Iran's retaliatory strike on Israel (2 killed, Mar 18) as a defined exchange rather than open-ended escalation. Iranian leadership uncertainty remains; Khamenei status still unresolved. Trump's coalition is absent; EU, Germany, Australia, Japan all refused — no de-escalation catalyst in sight. Iran's selective-access framing is not a reopening. Hold core long; working range $100-120, stress case $125+ if leadership collapse reinstates full blockade. PTTEP and Medco remain the cleanest ASEAN equity beta.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent at $102.52 on Mar 18; ASEAN Energy Equities proxy broadly flat. ASEAN Banks under continued inflation and subsidy risk pressure. Iran's retaliation against Israel on Mar 18 (2 killed) and the Joe Kent resignation signal deepening escalation on both military and political dimensions. Keep the position paired: Iran's bilateral-deal framing can still trigger intraday squeezes on ceasefire headlines, but the Iran–Israel exchange cycle structurally favours the stress/tail scenarios and energy over banks.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Renewed Oil Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP at 59.60 (xe.com Mar 18, slight firming). Brent at $102.52 keeps local fuel-inflation optics worsening. Iran's retaliatory strike on Israel on Mar 18 and Trump's absent coalition extend the oil-shock horizon; no ceasefire path visible. JKM at $19.27/MMBtu (carried Mar 17) — Philippines LNG import costs remain structurally elevated. Joe Kent resignation adds US political uncertainty. Retain the 60.00-61.00 stress target; stop discipline only if a credible accepted ceasefire appears.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Oil Import Stress Back in Play
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR at 16,963 (xe.com Mar 18) while Indonesia 10Y at 6.76% (est.) — Bank Indonesia in intervention mode not easing mode. Brent at $102.52 maintains structural oil-import pressure. Iran's retaliatory strike on Israel on Mar 18 deepens the escalation cycle; Iranian leadership uncertainty remains with Khamenei status unresolved. A leadership vacuum or coup scenario drives the IDR stress target to 17,500+ and tail above 18,000. Maintain the short-IDR bias.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG proxy at ~165bp on Mar 18 (0bp est.); ASEAN HY at ~515bp (0bp est.). Iran's retaliatory strike on Israel on Mar 18 (2 killed) and Trump's absent coalition maintain the leadership-uncertainty premium. Hold protection. The market will not re-tighten until both P&I cover returns AND Gulf attacks cease — neither condition in sight. 175-190bp remains the stress target. Trim only if oil re-prices below $90 for multiple sessions.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Keep Optionality
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM at $19.27/MMBtu (carried Mar 17 — Mar 18 not yet on public sources). TTF at ~€53.01/MWh (Mar 18 est. from $17.18/MMBtu oilprice.com). Iran's retaliatory strike on Israel on Mar 18 adds operational risk to bilateral transit deals — the only current source of any Hormuz LNG flow. Qatar partial restart continues; US LNG at capacity. JKM-over-TTF relative long; add on any near-term pullback given confirmed structural supply tightness.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's refinancing window remains effectively closed. Brent at $102.52 supports upstream cashflow eventually, but iTraxx Asia IG at ~165bp and ASEAN HY composite at ~515bp keep unsecured funding shut. Iran's retaliation on Israel on Mar 18 and Trump's absent coalition extend the risk-premium horizon indefinitely. Push immediately toward secured bank lines, private credit, and DFI-backed structures. Do not wait for market normalisation.

PTTEP should pre-fund now while Thailand 10Y remains at ~1.99% (est.) despite THB at 32.32 (xe.com Mar 18). Brent at $102.52 improves upstream EBITDA. Iran's retaliatory strike on Israel and Trump's absent coalition mean another leg higher is credible if Iranian leadership consolidates toward hardliners — pulling Thai inflation expectations up and closing the local-duration window. Execute the H2 2026 prefunding plan immediately and run a sanctions/compliance review on any Gulf-exposed JV structure.

Expand USD revolving-credit capacity now. At Brent $102.52, Wilmar's bunker and feedstock burden remains elevated with USD/SGD at 1.2766 (xe.com Mar 18). JKM at $19.27/MMBtu (carried Mar 17) — feedstock and energy cost pressure remains elevated. Iran's retaliatory strike on Israel on Mar 18 adds ongoing Gulf logistics risk. Use the still-functional IG bank market to extend tenor before another jump in freight or sanctions screening tightens trade-finance appetite.

Hyflux should be on an urgent restructuring track. Base probability is 18% and stress case is 52%, Brent is $102.52, and ASEAN HY is ~515bp. Iran's retaliatory strike on Israel on Mar 18 and the hardliner tail-risk scenario (30%) push any near-term market normalisation further out. That combination keeps the unsecured market effectively closed and worsens power-cost pressure on desalination assets. Engage private-credit and quasi-sovereign options now.
